items.briefing:
A newly identified Android malware called **RedHook** is making the rounds, and it's worth being aware of. It uses a wireless debugging feature built into Android devices to gain deep access without needing a physical connection. If your staff use Android phones for work, ensure wireless debugging is turned off in developer settings — most people won't have it on, but it's worth checking.

On the AI front, OpenAI recently ran workshops for Canberra public servants, a reminder that AI tools are increasingly entering Australian workplaces. If your organisation uses AI platforms, make sure staff aren't entering sensitive student or organisational data into them.

**Practical tip:** Check your Android phone's settings and confirm "Wireless Debugging" is disabled — go to Settings > Developer Options and turn it off.

featured.title: OpenAI runs AI workshop for public servants in Canberra
featured.quote: Australian government agencies directly testing AI capability for public service delivery and policy work.
